[2018-11-21 00:50] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Sure, but for me a unit's goodness changes throughout the game
[2018-11-21 00:51] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): On 6 Drones 6 Gold, Savior is the worst unit in the game
[2018-11-21 00:51] mrguy888: Okay but then "first x" is irrelevant since the units are not the same.
[2018-11-21 00:52] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So what's wrong about explaining to main that in a Savior vs Savior type of set it's ok to get 2nd Savior because it's still better than the alternative -- by saying that getting the best unit 2nd is still better than getting something worse?
[2018-11-21 00:53] mrguy888: Because people are making bad decisions and justifying them by talking about getting units first or second.
[2018-11-21 00:53] zera777 (Zera): Spirit, I noticed you have a custom avatar, so you must be a Centurion or Grand Centurion supporter, yes?
What is your avatar from?
[2018-11-21 00:54] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): That's an interesting point mrguy. I'll respond in #general_chat Zera
[2018-11-21 00:55] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So you're saying we shouldn't encourage that thinking by indulging and instead explain in a different way mrguy?
[2018-11-21 00:55] mrguy888: Yes, exactly. Explain it like it is.
[2018-11-21 00:58] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Ok, that sounds like a good approach to avoid misunderstanding
[2018-11-21 00:59] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But even though it may be misinterpreted, I didn't intend the misunderstood meaning
[2018-11-21 00:59] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): ```Getting unit first having advantage is applicable to most good units```
[2018-11-21 01:00] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): If both sides started with 3 gold and 1 engi, would you want to be p1 or p2?
[2018-11-21 01:01] mrguy888: I would want to be p1 because p1 has more stuff.
[2018-11-21 01:01] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Yea and p2 will still buy a Drone
[2018-11-21 01:01] mrguy888: P2 will resign, not buy anything.
[2018-11-21 01:02] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Sure, because it's easy to see you're losing
[2018-11-21 01:02] mrguy888: Talking about getting a unit first or second ignores the important fact that you always start your turn half a turn behind and end half a turn ahead of where your opponent is.
[2018-11-21 01:04] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): If I understand your point correctly, I think we're aligned
[2018-11-21 01:04] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): It's just a matter-of-fact thing
[2018-11-21 01:04] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): You buy the good unit, the game progresses
[2018-11-21 01:05] mrguy888: You are wording things in such a way that I don't think we are thinking the same way.
[2018-11-21 01:07] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I think we agree on everything :Lul:
[2018-11-21 01:07] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): And I concede that my wording has been confusing
[2018-11-21 01:07] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): And prone to misunderstanding
[2018-11-21 01:07] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): The thought behind my original point was that you won't just end your turn without doing anything
[2018-11-21 01:08] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Unless somehow that's the best play, but for the purpose of the Savior vs no Savior discussion, if the set warrants 2nd Savior, then you do it
[2018-11-21 01:08] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Where main seemed to think it's like a rule of thumb to not go 2nd Savior
[2018-11-21 01:08] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): And I tried to explain that by saying that if Savior is the best unit, you get it even if your opponent bought it first
[2018-11-21 01:09] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): You don't like that because it still implies that we should take into account who is getting it first, which may be true. But regardless I still think if you can choose to get it at the optimal timing first, then you have an advantage
[2018-11-21 01:10] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But yes, I agree that as discussed the optimal timing could be closer for my opponent due to p1/p2 half-turns
[2018-11-21 01:10] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): In which case they have the advantage
[2018-11-21 01:10] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Is there anything you disagree with in this summary?
[2018-11-21 01:12] mrguy888: Nope. The only part I disagreed with was the part you quoted earlier. I understood that to mean that getting a unit first is an advantage and you apply that thinking to most good units.
[2018-11-21 01:14] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): How about "the player who can get it closer to the optimal timing has an advantage"
[2018-11-21 01:17] apooche: Depends on what you mean by optimal
[2018-11-21 01:18] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Well problem is I feel like we have to assume one is omniscient and knows the outcome of the set
[2018-11-21 01:19] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But that'll almost never be the case
[2018-11-21 01:19] apooche: If optimal means most efficient/winning hardest, then you may want to get it multiple turns earlier
[2018-11-21 01:19] apooche: You want the first winning timing, not the timing that wins hardest
[2018-11-21 01:19] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So for practicality, I guess optimal means your educated guess as to when the unit provides the best return on investment out of all possible investments, or something like that
[2018-11-21 01:19] mrguy888: Winning hardest seems to be the best definition for optimal since the goal is winning.
[2018-11-21 01:20] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Yea but this feels similar to the first/second argument which was bad due to misunderstanding, no?
[2018-11-21 01:20] apooche: Well sure I mean you can get 22 engi antima every game right
[2018-11-21 01:20] mrguy888: But the specifics of what you do with your half turn advantage and how you set up your timing for the units makes the optimal timing very muddy to the point where player advantage is not relevant to the outcome of the actual game.
[2018-11-21 01:21] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Though maybe you can say getting it earlier is a worse return on investment than later which may solve that issue
[2018-11-21 01:21] mrguy888: Optimal accounts for optimal play by opponent as well. 22 engi antima is out.
[2018-11-21 01:23] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Ok I think that gave me the conclusion mrguy
[2018-11-21 01:25] mrguy888: Every turn matters, before and after buying the unit in question as well as what else you do on the turn you buy the unit in question. Anything that simplifies those factors out is bad thinking.
[2018-11-21 01:25] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): The true optimal timing is tough to pinpoint practically, but if you were able to do it then you can say that the side who can get the best unit at the optimal timing has an advantage, but at that point you have probably solved the set in which case this observation essentially means nothing

[2018-11-22 08:39] Deleted User (Deleted User): >Last night we started to play this game for the first time. 2 men who had no idea what we were doing at the beginning of the game. As time passed we started figuring things out, one dude in particular started buying up Tarsier and Amporilla cards. I thought nothing of it 'cause I was buying up Drone cards, what a friend and I called "douches". At the end of the game I was sure I was gonna win 'cause I had s shitton of drones but then it turns out that Amporilla is, apparently, worth 1 Tarsier card per 1 Tarsiers in the deck PER Amporilla card. He had something like 4 Amporilla cards, so if he had 4 tarsiers then he had 4 Tarsier points x the 4 Amporilla cards. Which if course is a lot.

Is that how Amporilla works? I was under the impression that it was 1 Tarsier per 1 Tarsier ONLY and after those tarsiers were used, the extra Amporilla cards were useless. Because if Amporilla actually works like that, then what the hell is the point of doing anything else? All I could do is buy Amporilla and fatten up my Tarsiers and that'll be the end of the game, right?

```Pay Cap   
One possible solution for publishers who claim their games don’t rely on exposing addictive personalities to open ended spending, or who wish to end it, is to put a cap on player expenditure – after which a player “gets everything.” This could be a one-time cap or a monthly cap – and it could be set wherever the publisher wants provided it is visible to the player.    
With this in place we as players could more easily see what is going on. We would know that a Match-3 game with a cap of $30 was likely less abusive than one that set a cap at $3000 a month.```
